parameters.content:
# HMZ - Reply Error Handler - Validation (Phase 4A)

WHAT: Begins with an n8n Error Trigger (any other Phase 4A workflow execution failure) and an Execute Workflow Trigger for synthetic tests. Normalises workflow/execution/failed-node/intake/send-key/send-state/HTTP-status/error-class/attempt/retryable/operator-action fields, redacts secrets/Authorization/full payloads/long PII, persists the sanitised record to the hmz-send-state sidecar, and returns a placeholder notification object only.

NEVER: calls Slack, email, Sheets, or any other external service. NEVER: converts a SEND_UNCERTAIN record into a retry (retryable is forced false whenever send_state=SEND_UNCERTAIN).

SIDECAR: http://hmz-send-state:5681 (internal Docker network only).

INACTIVE: this workflow must remain inactive after import and after every test.

## A-D. Error Record Pipeline

A accepts either a real Error Trigger item ($json.execution / $json.workflow / $json.trigger.error) or a synthetic Execute Workflow Trigger item (flat fields: workflow_id, workflow_name, execution_id, failed_node, intake_id, send_key, send_state, http_status, attempt) and derives error_class / retryable / operator_action deterministically (400=PERMANENT_FAILURE, 401/402/403=AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE, 404=INVALID_REPLY_TARGET, 429/5xx=RETRYABLE, send_state=SEND_UNCERTAIN=>SEND_UNCERTAIN with retryable forced false).

B redacts any key matching authorization/api-key/secret/token/password/cookie/bearer and truncates strings over 300 chars. C persists the sanitised error_record via POST hmz-send-state /v1/error (internal network only, no credentials). D returns a placeholder notification object only - never Slack/email/Sheets.

parameters.content:
# HMZ - Reply SLA Watchdog - Validation (Phase 4B)

WHAT: Periodically (Schedule Trigger, eventual) or on demand (Execute Workflow Trigger, synthetic tests) reads every unfinished hmz-send-state send/error record from GET /v1/unfinished, classifies each into a watchdog category (AI_CLASSIFICATION_WAIT, API_RETRY, HUMAN_REVIEW, SEND_FAILURE, SEND_UNCERTAIN, SUPPRESSION_FAILURE, UNKNOWN_STATE), and compares its age against the WARNING (180s) and BREACH (300s) thresholds.

SLOs: only SEND_UNCERTAIN records belong to the Transmission SLO (an attempted send awaiting reconciliation). Every other category - including every HUMAN_REVIEW item - belongs to the Processing SLO. A draft, queue item, approval request, or human-review item is never counted as transmitted.

ALERTING: warning/breach records are reduced to sanitised alert candidates (category + SLO type + record identifier + status) and deduplicated atomically via POST /v1/alert/dedupe before routing to a PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED notification object only - no real notification call.

VALIDATION MODE: hardcoded (watchdog_result.validation_mode = true). SIDECAR: http://hmz-send-state:5681 (internal Docker network only).

INACTIVE: this workflow must remain inactive after import and after every test.

parameters.jsCode: return $input.all();

parameters.content:
## C-J. Evaluation, Alerting, Persistence

C merges GET /v1/unfinished (sends/errors) onto the triggering item, preserving any test_overrides.now / synthetic flag from the Execute Workflow Trigger so synthetic runs are deterministic. D classifies and ages every record (warning_threshold_seconds=180, breach_threshold_seconds=300) and splits Processing vs Transmission SLO totals.

E builds one sanitised alert candidate per warning/breach record (stable alert_key = category + SLO type + kind + identifier + status, no message bodies, no raw email addresses - identifiers are already sha256 hashes). F atomically records each alert_key at most once via POST /v1/alert/dedupe (open('wx')); G merges the dedupe response so a repeated alert is never re-notified.

H builds the final sanitised watchdog_result (validation_mode=true, notification.surface=PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED, delivered=false - no real notification call). I persists it via POST /v1/phase4b/result; J attaches the returned resultId.

parameters.content:
# HMZ - Reply Full Test Harness - Validation (Phase 4B)

WHAT: On demand (Manual Trigger or Execute Workflow Trigger), runs the complete Phase 4 synthetic fixture matrix (60 fixtures across intake/policy, sender/suppression, failure-policy, and monitoring/error-handling groups) against the deterministic Phase 4A/4B core modules (sender-core, error-core, watchdog-core, harness-core) embedded directly into this workflow. Every fixture is synthetic (synthetic: true); no fixture ever calls Instantly or uses credentials.

ASSERTIONS: each fixture declares a `check` (which deterministic core function(s) to run) and an `expected` object compared by dot-path against the actual result. harness_result.overall_result is FAIL if any fixture fails.

LOCAL CONTRACT: classify_intake fixtures exercise classifyIntakeEvent() - a local deterministic reimplementation of the safety-critical subset of HMZ_APPROVED_REPLY_RULES.md sections 3 and 5, used because no shared decision-core module exists for workflows 01/02. Two monitoring fixtures (sidecar_resolved_exclusion, sidecar_alert_dedupe) report skipped:true here and are proven directly against infrastructure/send-state/state-store.mjs by run-offline-tests.mjs. Full sub-workflow integration (Execute Workflow calls into workflows 01-06 by ID) is recorded as a Phase 5 task.

VALIDATION MODE: hardcoded (validation_mode = true, synthetic = true). SIDECAR: http://hmz-send-state:5681 (internal Docker network only, used only to persist the sanitised harness_result).

INACTIVE: this workflow must remain inactive after import and after every test.

const SENDER_CONFIG = {"OPERATING_MODE": "VALIDATION", "DRY_RUN": true, "LIVE_CAMPAIGNS": [], "LIVE_CREDENTIAL_READY": false};

const SEND_RESULT_STATES = {"SENT": "SENT", "PERMANENT_FAILURE": "PERMANENT_FAILURE", "AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE": "AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE", "INVALID_REPLY_TARGET": "INVALID_REPLY_TARGET", "RETRY_EXHAUSTED": "RETRY_EXHAUSTED", "SEND_UNCERTAIN": "SEND_UNCERTAIN"};

const MAX_SEND_ATTEMPTS = 3;

const BASE_BACKOFF_MS = 100;

const MAX_BACKOFF_MS = 2000;

const RETRY_AFTER_CAP_MS = 5000;

const RECONCILE_STATES = {"SENT_RECONCILED": "SENT_RECONCILED", "HUMAN_REVIEW_ZERO_MATCHES": "HUMAN_REVIEW_ZERO_MATCHES", "HUMAN_REVIEW_MULTIPLE_MATCHES": "HUMAN_REVIEW_MULTIPLE_MATCHES"};

function normalizeEmail(value) {
  return String(value ?? '').trim().toLowerCase();
}

function djb2Hash(str) {
  let hash = 5381;
  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) {
    hash = (hash << 5) + hash + str.charCodeAt(i);
    hash = hash & 0xffffffff;
  }
  return (hash >>> 0).toString(16).padStart(8, '0');
}

function deriveSendKey({ inboundEmailId, sender, recipient, policyTemplateId }) {
  const raw = [
    'HMZ_PHASE4A_SEND',
    String(inboundEmailId ?? '').trim(),
    normalizeEmail(sender),
    normalizeEmail(recipient),
    String(policyTemplateId ?? '').trim(),
  ].join('|');
  return `send-${djb2Hash(raw)}`;
}

function isValidSentContract(body) {
  return (
    !!body &&
    typeof body === 'object' &&
    body.status === 'sent' &&
    typeof body.messageId === 'string' &&
    body.messageId.length > 0
  );
}

function deterministicJitter(attemptNumber) {
  return (attemptNumber * 37) % 50;
}

function validateSenderInput(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const nes = input.nes || {};
  const decision = input.decision || {};
  const validation = input.validation || {};
  const threading = nes.threading || {};

  const errors = [];

  if (validation.valid !== true) errors.push('validation.valid is not true');
  if (!threading.reply_to_uuid) errors.push('nes.threading.reply_to_uuid (inbound email id) is missing');
  if (!nes.eaccount || nes.eaccount === 'UNKNOWN') errors.push('nes.eaccount (sender) is missing or UNKNOWN');
  if (!nes.lead_email || nes.lead_email === 'UNKNOWN') errors.push('nes.lead_email (recipient) is missing or UNKNOWN');
  if (!nes.intake_id) errors.push('nes.intake_id is missing');
  if (typeof decision.reply_permitted !== 'boolean') errors.push('decision.reply_permitted is missing');
  if (decision.human_review_required !== true) errors.push('decision.human_review_required is not true');

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    sender_validation: {
      valid: errors.length === 0,
      errors,
    },
  });
}

function runSendGates(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const cfg = SENDER_CONFIG;
  const reasons = [];

  if (input.sender_validation && input.sender_validation.valid !== true) {
    reasons.push('sender_validation_failed');
  }

  const configGatePassed =
    cfg.OPERATING_MODE === 'VALIDATION' &&
    cfg.DRY_RUN === true &&
    Array.isArray(cfg.LIVE_CAMPAIGNS) &&
    cfg.LIVE_CAMPAIGNS.length === 0;
  if (!configGatePassed) reasons.push('config_gate_failed');

  const approval = input.approval || {};
  const approvalGatePassed = approval.approved === true;
  if (!approvalGatePassed) reasons.push('approval_missing');

  const nes = input.nes || {};
  const campaignId = nes.campaign_id || null;
  const liveCampaignSendAllowed = cfg.LIVE_CAMPAIGNS.includes(campaignId);
  if (!liveCampaignSendAllowed) reasons.push('live_campaigns_empty_live_send_blocked');

  const senderGatePassed = !!nes.eaccount && nes.eaccount !== 'UNKNOWN';
  if (!senderGatePassed) reasons.push('sender_gate_failed');

  const draft = input.draft || {};
  const draftText = typeof draft.draft_text === 'string' ? draft.draft_text : null;
  const unresolvedTokens = draftText ? draftText.match(/\[\[[^\]]+\]\]|<<[^>]+>>/g) || [] : [];
  const draftVariableGatePassed = draftText === null || unresolvedTokens.length === 0;
  if (!draftVariableGatePassed) reasons.push('unresolved_template_variables');

  const decision = input.decision || {};
  const suppressionRequired =
    decision.address_suppression_intent === 'ORGANISATION_DNC' ||
    decision.address_suppression_intent === 'GLOBAL_BLOCKLIST' ||
    decision.durable_dnc_intent === true;

  const dryRunGatePassed = cfg.DRY_RUN === true;
  if (!dryRunGatePassed) reasons.push('dry_run_gate_failed');

  const passed =
    (input.sender_validation ? input.sender_validation.valid === true : true) &&
    configGatePassed &&
    approvalGatePassed &&
    senderGatePassed &&
    draftVariableGatePassed &&
    dryRunGatePassed;

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    gates: {
      config_gate_passed: configGatePassed,
      approval_gate_passed: approvalGatePassed,
      live_campaign_send_allowed: liveCampaignSendAllowed,
      sender_gate_passed: senderGatePassed,
      draft_variable_gate_passed: draftVariableGatePassed,
      unresolved_tokens: unresolvedTokens,
      suppression_required: suppressionRequired,
      dry_run_gate_passed: dryRunGatePassed,
      passed,
      reasons,
    },
  });
}

function buildGateRejectionTerminal(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const gates = input.gates || {};
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    terminal: {
      schema_version: '1.0',
      result: 'BLOCKED',
      send_state: 'BLOCKED',
      reason: 'send_gates_failed',
      details: gates.reasons || [],
      sent: false,
    },
  });
}

function computeSendKey(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const nes = input.nes || {};
  const threading = nes.threading || {};
  const decision = input.decision || {};

  const inboundEmailId = threading.reply_to_uuid || null;
  const sender = nes.eaccount || null;
  const recipient = nes.lead_email || null;
  const policyTemplateId = decision.reply_template_id || 'NO_TEMPLATE';

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    send_identity: {
      inboundEmailId,
      sender,
      recipient,
      policyTemplateId,
      sendKey: deriveSendKey({ inboundEmailId, sender, recipient, policyTemplateId }),
    },
  });
}

function normalizeAcquisitionResult(priorItem, acquireResponse) {
  const input = priorItem || {};
  const response = acquireResponse || {};
  const acquired = response.acquired === true;

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    acquisition: {
      acquired,
      blocked: !acquired,
      reason: response.reason || null,
      priorState: response.priorState || null,
      sendKey: response.sendKey || (input.send_identity && input.send_identity.sendKey) || null,
    },
  });
}

function buildBlockedDuplicateTerminal(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const acquisition = input.acquisition || {};
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    terminal: {

}

function mockSuppressionAdapter(item) {

function verifySuppressionResults(item) {

function buildDryRunTerminal(priorItem, transitionResponse) {

  const decision = input.decision || {};

function classifySendOutcome(outcome) {
  const out = outcome || {};

  if (out.kind === 'http') {
    const status = out.status;
    const body = out.body;
    const headers = out.headers || {};

    if (status === 400) {
      return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.PERMANENT_FAILURE, ambiguousSideEffect: false });
    }
    if (status === 401 || status === 402 || status === 403) {
      return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE, ambiguousSideEffect: false });
    }
    if (status === 404) {
      return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.INVALID_REPLY_TARGET, ambiguousSideEffect: false });
    }
    if (status === 429 || status === 500 || status === 502 || status === 503 || status === 504) {
      const retryAfterRaw = headers['retry-after'] ?? headers['Retry-After'] ?? null;
      const retryAfterSeconds = retryAfterRaw == null ? null : Number(retryAfterRaw);
      const retryAfterMs =
        status === 429 && Number.isFinite(retryAfterSeconds) && retryAfterSeconds >= 0
          ? retryAfterSeconds * 1000
          : null;
      return Object.assign({}, {
        mode: 'retryable',
        exhaustedState: SEND_RESULT_STATES.RETRY_EXHAUSTED,
        retryAfterMs,
        ambiguousSideEffect: false,
      });
    }
    if (status >= 200 && status < 300) {
      if (isValidSentContract(body)) {
        return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.SENT, ambiguousSideEffect: false });
      }
      return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.SEND_UNCERTAIN, ambiguousSideEffect: true });
    }
    return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.SEND_UNCERTAIN, ambiguousSideEffect: true });
  }

  if (out.kind === 'network-error') {
    const retryablePhases = ['connection-refused', 'pre-submission'];
    if (retryablePhases.includes(out.errorPhase)) {
      return Object.assign({}, {
        mode: 'retryable',
        exhaustedState: SEND_RESULT_STATES.RETRY_EXHAUSTED,
        retryAfterMs: null,
        ambiguousSideEffect: false,
      });
    }
    return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.SEND_UNCERTAIN, ambiguousSideEffect: true });
  }

  return Object.assign({}, { mode: 'terminal', state: SEND_RESULT_STATES.SEND_UNCERTAIN, ambiguousSideEffect: true });
}

function planSendAttempts(outcomes, options) {
  const opts = options || {};
  const jitter = opts.jitter || deterministicJitter;
  const maxAttempts = opts.maxAttempts || MAX_SEND_ATTEMPTS;
  const list = Array.isArray(outcomes) ? outcomes : [];

  const attemptOutcomes = [];
  const backoffDelaysRequested = [];
  let postSubmissions = 0;
  let retried = false;
  let retryAfterHonoured = null;
  let finalState = null;
  let secondPostAfterUncertain = false;

  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= Math.min(maxAttempts, list.length || maxAttempts); attempt++) {
    const outcome = list[attempt - 1] || { kind: 'network-error', errorPhase: 'pre-submission', serverReceived: false };
    attemptOutcomes.push(outcome);
    if (outcome.serverReceived) postSubmissions++;

    const classification = classifySendOutcome(outcome);

    if (classification.mode === 'terminal') {
      finalState = classification.state;
      if (finalState === SEND_RESULT_STATES.SEND_UNCERTAIN && attempt < list.length) {
        secondPostAfterUncertain = true;
      }
      break;
    }

    if (attempt === maxAttempts || attempt === list.length) {
      finalState = classification.exhaustedState;
      break;
    }

    let delayMs;
    if (classification.retryAfterMs != null) {
      delayMs = Math.min(classification.retryAfterMs, RETRY_AFTER_CAP_MS);
      if (retryAfterHonoured === null) retryAfterHonoured = true;
    } else {
      if (retryAfterHonoured === null) retryAfterHonoured = false;
      const exponential = BASE_BACKOFF_MS * 2 ** (attempt - 1);
      delayMs = Math.min(exponential + jitter(attempt), MAX_BACKOFF_MS);
    }
    backoffDelaysRequested.push(delayMs);
    retried = true;
  }

  return Object.assign({}, {
    finalState,
    attempts: attemptOutcomes.length,
    postSubmissions,
    retried,
    backoffDelaysRequested,
    retryAfterHonoured,
    secondPostAfterUncertain,
    humanReviewRequired: finalState !== SEND_RESULT_STATES.SENT,
  });
}

function reconcileMatches(checks, options) {
  const opts = options || {};
  const consecutiveRequired = opts.consecutiveRequired || 2;
  const polls = Array.isArray(checks) ? checks : [];

  let lastSingleMatchId = null;
  let consecutiveSameSingleMatch = 0;

  for (const matchIds of polls) {
    const ids = Array.isArray(matchIds) ? matchIds : [];
    if (ids.length > 1) {
      return Object.assign({}, { state: RECONCILE_STATES.HUMAN_REVIEW_MULTIPLE_MATCHES, matchCount: ids.length, matchIds: ids });
    }
    if (ids.length === 1) {
      const currentId = ids[0];
      if (currentId === lastSingleMatchId) {
        consecutiveSameSingleMatch += 1;
      } else {
        lastSingleMatchId = currentId;
        consecutiveSameSingleMatch = 1;
      }
      if (consecutiveSameSingleMatch >= consecutiveRequired) {
        return Object.assign({}, { state: RECONCILE_STATES.SENT_RECONCILED, matchCount: 1, matchId: currentId });
      }
    } else {
      lastSingleMatchId = null;
      consecutiveSameSingleMatch = 0;
    }
  }

  return Object.assign({}, { state: RECONCILE_STATES.HUMAN_REVIEW_ZERO_MATCHES, matchCount: 0, matchIds: [] });
}

const REDACT_KEY_PATTERN = /(authoriz|api[_-]?key|secret|token|password|cookie|bearer)/i;

const MAX_STRING_LENGTH = 300;

const RETRYABLE_HTTP_STATUSES = [429, 500, 502, 503, 504];

const OPERATOR_ACTION_BY_CLASS = {"PERMANENT_FAILURE": "REVIEW_PAYLOAD", "AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE": "CHECK_CREDENTIALS_AND_PLAN", "INVALID_REPLY_TARGET": "INVESTIGATE_REPLY_TARGET", "RETRYABLE": "MONITOR_RETRY", "SEND_UNCERTAIN": "MANUAL_RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED", "UNKNOWN": "MANUAL_REVIEW"};

function redactValue(value) {
  if (value === null || value === undefined) return value;
  if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.slice(0, 20).map(redactValue);
  if (typeof value === 'object') {
    const out = {};
    for (const key of Object.keys(value)) {
      if (REDACT_KEY_PATTERN.test(key)) {
        out[key] = '<REDACTED>';
        continue;
      }
      out[key] = redactValue(value[key]);
    }
    return out;
  }
  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.length > MAX_STRING_LENGTH) {
    return `${value.slice(0, MAX_STRING_LENGTH)}...<TRUNCATED>`;
  }
  return value;
}

function classifyErrorClass(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  if (input.error_class) return input.error_class;

  // SEND_UNCERTAIN is a definitive ambiguous-outcome determination made by
  // the Sender workflow and must take priority over any http_status-based
  // inference, so error_class/retryable/operator_action stay consistent
  // (never RETRYABLE/MONITOR_RETRY for a SEND_UNCERTAIN record).
  if (input.send_state === 'SEND_UNCERTAIN') return 'SEND_UNCERTAIN';

  const status = input.http_status;
  if (status === 400) return 'PERMANENT_FAILURE';
  if (status === 401 || status === 402 || status === 403) return 'AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE';
  if (status === 404) return 'INVALID_REPLY_TARGET';
  if (RETRYABLE_HTTP_STATUSES.includes(status)) return 'RETRYABLE';
  return 'UNKNOWN';
}

function deriveRetryable(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  if (input.send_state === 'SEND_UNCERTAIN') return false;
  if (typeof input.retryable === 'boolean') return input.retryable;
  return classifyErrorClass(input) === 'RETRYABLE';
}

function deriveOperatorAction(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  if (input.operator_action) return input.operator_action;
  const errorClass = classifyErrorClass(input);
  return OPERATOR_ACTION_BY_CLASS[errorClass] || 'MANUAL_REVIEW';
}

function normalizeErrorEvent(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const execution = input.execution || {};
  const workflowInfo = input.workflow || {};
  const trigger = input.trigger || {};
  const triggerError = trigger.error || {};

  const failedNode =
    input.failed_node || triggerError.node?.name || execution.lastNodeExecuted || null;

  const errorClass = classifyErrorClass(input);

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    error_record: {
      schema_version: '1.0',
      workflow_id: workflowInfo.id || input.workflow_id || null,
      workflow_name: workflowInfo.name || input.workflow_name || null,
      execution_id: execution.id || input.execution_id || null,
      failed_node: failedNode,
      intake_id: input.intake_id || (input.nes && input.nes.intake_id) || null,
      send_key:
        input.send_key ||
        (input.acquisition && input.acquisition.sendKey) ||
        (input.send_identity && input.send_identity.sendKey) ||
        null,
      send_state: input.send_state || (input.terminal && input.terminal.send_state) || null,
      http_status: typeof input.http_status === 'number' ? input.http_status : null,
      error_class: errorClass,
      attempt: typeof input.attempt === 'number' ? input.attempt : null,
      retryable: deriveRetryable(input),
      operator_action: deriveOperatorAction(input),
    },
  });
}

function redactErrorRecord(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    error_record: redactValue(input.error_record || {}),
  });
}

function buildPlaceholderNotification(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const record = input.error_record || {};
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    notification: {
      schema_version: '1.0',
      surface: 'PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED',
      delivered: false,
      summary: `Error in ${record.workflow_name || 'unknown workflow'}: ${record.error_class || 'UNKNOWN'} at node ${record.failed_node || 'unknown'}`,
      operator_action: record.operator_action || 'MANUAL_REVIEW',
    },
  });
}

function attachErrorId(priorItem, persistResponse) {
  const input = priorItem || {};
  const response = persistResponse || {};
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    persisted_error: {
      errorId: response.errorId || null,
    },
  });
}

const SEND_STATE_BASE_URL = "http://hmz-send-state:5681";

const SLA_WARNING_THRESHOLD_SECONDS = 180;

const SLA_BREACH_THRESHOLD_SECONDS = 300;

const WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES = {"AI_CLASSIFICATION_WAIT": "AI_CLASSIFICATION_WAIT", "API_RETRY": "API_RETRY", "HUMAN_REVIEW": "HUMAN_REVIEW", "SEND_FAILURE": "SEND_FAILURE", "SEND_UNCERTAIN": "SEND_UNCERTAIN", "SUPPRESSION_FAILURE": "SUPPRESSION_FAILURE", "UNKNOWN_STATE": "UNKNOWN_STATE"};

const TRANSMISSION_CATEGORIES = ["SEND_UNCERTAIN"];

const SLO_TYPES = {"PROCESSING": "PROCESSING", "TRANSMISSION": "TRANSMISSION"};

const SLA_STATUSES = {"OK": "OK", "WARNING": "WARNING", "BREACH": "BREACH"};

const SEND_FAILURE_STATES = ["PERMANENT_FAILURE", "AUTH_OR_PLAN_FAILURE", "INVALID_REPLY_TARGET", "RETRY_EXHAUSTED"];

function classifySendRecord(record) {
  const rec = record || {};
  switch (rec.state) {
    case 'LOCKED':
      return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.AI_CLASSIFICATION_WAIT;
    case 'SUBMITTING':
      return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.API_RETRY;
    case 'SENT':
    case 'SEND_UNCERTAIN':
      return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.SEND_UNCERTAIN;
    default:
      return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.UNKNOWN_STATE;
  }
}

function classifyErrorRecord(record) {
  const rec = record || {};
  const errorClass = rec.error_class;

  if (errorClass === 'SEND_UNCERTAIN') return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.SEND_UNCERTAIN;
  if (errorClass === 'RETRYABLE') return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.API_RETRY;
  if (SEND_FAILURE_STATES.includes(errorClass)) return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.SEND_FAILURE;
  if (rec.reason === 'suppression_verification_failed') return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.SUPPRESSION_FAILURE;
  if (rec.operator_action === 'MANUAL_REVIEW' || errorClass === 'UNKNOWN') {
    return rec.reason === undefined && errorClass === 'UNKNOWN'
      ? WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.UNKNOWN_STATE
      : WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.HUMAN_REVIEW;
  }
  return WATCHDOG_CATEGORIES.UNKNOWN_STATE;
}

function sloTypeForCategory(category) {
  return TRANSMISSION_CATEGORIES.includes(category) ? SLO_TYPES.TRANSMISSION : SLO_TYPES.PROCESSING;
}

function timestampForSendRecord(record, sloType) {
  const rec = record || {};
  const details = rec.details || {};
  if (sloType === SLO_TYPES.TRANSMISSION) {
    return details.transmissionStartedAt || details.submissionStartedAt || rec.updatedAt || rec.createdAt || null;
  }
  return rec.createdAt || rec.updatedAt || null;
}

function ageSeconds(timestampIso, nowIso) {
  const ts = Date.parse(timestampIso || '');
  const now = Date.parse(nowIso || '');
  if (!Number.isFinite(ts) || !Number.isFinite(now)) return null;
  return Math.max(0, Math.round((now - ts) / 1000));
}

function statusForAge(age) {
  if (age === null) return SLA_STATUSES.OK;
  if (age >= SLA_BREACH_THRESHOLD_SECONDS) return SLA_STATUSES.BREACH;
  if (age >= SLA_WARNING_THRESHOLD_SECONDS) return SLA_STATUSES.WARNING;
  return SLA_STATUSES.OK;
}

function evaluateUnfinishedRecords(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const unfinished = input.unfinished || { sends: [], errors: [] };
  const now = (input.test_overrides && input.test_overrides.now) || input.now || new Date().toISOString();

  const records = [];

  for (const send of unfinished.sends || []) {
    const category = classifySendRecord(send);
    const sloType = sloTypeForCategory(category);
    const ageTimestamp = timestampForSendRecord(send, sloType);
    const age = ageSeconds(ageTimestamp, now);
    records.push({
      kind: 'send',
      identifier: send.sendKey || null,
      state: send.state || null,
      category,
      sloType,
      ageSeconds: age,
      status: statusForAge(age),
      createdAt: send.createdAt || null,
      updatedAt: send.updatedAt || null,
      ageTimestamp,
    });
  }

  for (const error of unfinished.errors || []) {
    const category = classifyErrorRecord(error);
    const sloType = sloTypeForCategory(category);
    const age = ageSeconds(error.recordedAt, now);
    records.push({
      kind: 'error',
      identifier: error.errorId || null,
      state: error.error_class || null,
      category,
      sloType,
      ageSeconds: age,
      status: statusForAge(age),
      updatedAt: error.recordedAt || null,
    });
  }

  const warnings = records.filter((r) => r.status === SLA_STATUSES.WARNING);
  const breaches = records.filter((r) => r.status === SLA_STATUSES.BREACH);

  const processing = records.filter((r) => r.sloType === SLO_TYPES.PROCESSING);
  const transmission = records.filter((r) => r.sloType === SLO_TYPES.TRANSMISSION);

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    watchdog_evaluation: {
      schema_version: '1.0',
      synthetic: input.synthetic === true,
      now,
      warning_threshold_seconds: SLA_WARNING_THRESHOLD_SECONDS,
      breach_threshold_seconds: SLA_BREACH_THRESHOLD_SECONDS,
      records,
      warnings,
      breaches,
      processing_slo: {
        total: processing.length,
        warnings: processing.filter((r) => r.status === SLA_STATUSES.WARNING).length,
        breaches: processing.filter((r) => r.status === SLA_STATUSES.BREACH).length,
      },
      transmission_slo: {
        total: transmission.length,
        warnings: transmission.filter((r) => r.status === SLA_STATUSES.WARNING).length,
        breaches: transmission.filter((r) => r.status === SLA_STATUSES.BREACH).length,
      },
    },
  });
}

function buildAlertKey(record) {
  const rec = record || {};
  return ['WATCHDOG_ALERT', rec.sloType, rec.category, rec.kind, rec.identifier, rec.status].join('|');
}

function buildAlertRecords(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const evaluation = input.watchdog_evaluation || { warnings: [], breaches: [] };
  const candidates = [...(evaluation.warnings || []), ...(evaluation.breaches || [])];

  const alerts = candidates.map((record) => ({
    schema_version: '1.0',
    alert_key: buildAlertKey(record),
    severity: record.status,
    sloType: record.sloType,
    category: record.category,
    kind: record.kind,
    identifier: record.identifier,
    ageSeconds: record.ageSeconds,
  }));

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    alert_candidates: alerts,
  });
}

function mergeAlertDedupeResults(priorItem, dedupeResponse) {
  const input = priorItem || {};
  const candidates = input.alert_candidates || [];
  const response = dedupeResponse || {};
  if (!Array.isArray(response.results) || response.results.length !== candidates.length) {
    throw new Error('invalid_alert_dedupe_response');
  }
  const results = response.results;
  for (const result of results) {
    if (typeof result?.deduped !== 'boolean') {
      throw new Error('invalid_alert_dedupe_item');
    }
  }

  const alerts = candidates.map((candidate, idx) => {
    const dedupe = results[idx];
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...candidate,
      deduped: dedupe.deduped === true,
      first_seen_at: dedupe.firstSeenAt || null,
    });
  });

  const toNotify = alerts.filter((a) => a.deduped !== true);

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    alerts,
    notification: {
      schema_version: '1.0',
      surface: 'PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED',
      delivered: false,
      alert_count: toNotify.length,
      deduped_count: alerts.length - toNotify.length,
    },
  });
}

function buildWatchdogResult(item) {
  const input = item || {};
  const evaluation = input.watchdog_evaluation || {};
  const alerts = input.alerts || [];
  const notification = input.notification || { surface: 'PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED', delivered: false };

  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    watchdog_result: {
      schema_version: '1.0',
      synthetic: evaluation.synthetic === true,
      validation_mode: true,
      now: evaluation.now || null,
      processing_slo: evaluation.processing_slo || { total: 0, warnings: 0, breaches: 0 },
      transmission_slo: evaluation.transmission_slo || { total: 0, warnings: 0, breaches: 0 },
      alerts: alerts.map((a) => ({
        alert_key: a.alert_key,
        severity: a.severity,
        sloType: a.sloType,
        category: a.category,
        kind: a.kind,
        identifier: a.identifier,
        ageSeconds: a.ageSeconds,
        deduped: a.deduped === true,
      })),
      notification,
    },
  });
}

function mergeUnfinishedResponse(priorItem, unfinishedResponse) {
  const input = priorItem || {};
  const response = unfinishedResponse || {};
  if (!Array.isArray(response.sends) || !Array.isArray(response.errors)) {
    throw new Error('invalid_unfinished_response');
  }
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    unfinished: {
      sends: response.sends,
      errors: response.errors,
    },
  });
}

function attachPhase4bResultId(priorItem, persistResponse) {
  const input = priorItem || {};
  const response = persistResponse || {};
  if (!/^[0-9a-f]{16}$/.test(String(response.resultId || ''))) {
    throw new Error('invalid_phase4b_persist_response');
  }
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...input,
    phase4b_result_id: response.resultId,
  });
}

const CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS = {"T1": {"reply_mode": "AI_DRAFT_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T2": {"reply_mode": "AI_DRAFT_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T3": {"reply_mode": "FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T4": {"reply_mode": "FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T5": {"reply_mode": "FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T6": {"reply_mode": "FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false, "interest_status_update": "NOT_INTERESTED"}, "T7": {"reply_mode": "FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "WORKSPACE_DNC", "human_review_required": false, "escalation_required": false}, "T8": {"reply_mode": "NO_REPLY", "stop_sequence": false, "pause_sequence": true, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": false, "escalation_required": false}, "T9": {"reply_mode": "NO_REPLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": false, "escalation_required": false, "data_cleanup_required": true}, "T10": {"reply_mode": "FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T11": {"reply_mode": "HUMAN_ONLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T12": {"reply_mode": "HUMAN_ONLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "REVIEW_HOLD", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": true}, "T13": {"reply_mode": "HUMAN_ONLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "REVIEW_HOLD", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": true}, "T14": {"reply_mode": "HUMAN_ONLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T15": {"reply_mode": "HUMAN_ONLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}, "T16": {"reply_mode": "HUMAN_ONLY", "stop_sequence": true, "pause_sequence": false, "suppression_level": "NONE", "human_review_required": true, "escalation_required": false}};

const RULES_HARD_SAFETY = [{"code": "det-unsub-001", "re": /(unsubscribe|remove me|take me off|opt out|stop emailing|stop contacting|do not contact me again|delete me from your outreach)/, "category": "T7"}, {"code": "det-legal-001", "re": /(gdpr|ccpa|hipaa|privacy act|data protection|right to be forgotten|where did you get my (email|data|info))/, "category": "T12", "legal": true, "privacy": true}, {"code": "det-legal-002", "re": /(attorney|lawyer|legal counsel|lawsuit|cease and desist|c&d)/, "category": "T12", "legal": true}, {"code": "det-regulator-001", "re": /(ftc|\bico\b|reporting you to|filing a complaint with)/, "category": "T12", "legal": true}, {"code": "det-hostile-001", "re": /(i'?m going to (find|hurt) you|i know where you (live|work)|extort|doxx)/, "category": "T13", "legal": true, "reputational": true}, {"code": "det-hostile-002", "re": /(fuck|asshole|idiot|you (will|are going to) regret)/, "category": "T13", "reputational": true}, {"code": "det-complaint-001", "re": /(spam|reported you|reporting this to|continued contact after)/, "category": "T12", "reputational": true}];

const RULES_BOUNCE_OOO = [{"code": "det-bounce-001", "re": /(mailer-daemon|postmaster)/, "category": "T9", "needsEvidence": true}, {"code": "det-bounce-002", "re": /(undeliverable|delivery failed|returned mail|mailbox full)/, "category": "T9", "needsEvidence": true}, {"code": "det-ooo-001", "re": /(out of office|on vacation|on (annual |parental )?leave|sabbatical|automatic reply|away from the office)/, "category": "T8"}];

const RULES_PRICE_ATTACH = [{"code": "det-price-001", "re": /(your price|your cost|quote|\brfp\b|\brfi\b|\bcontract\b|\bmsa\b|\bsow\b|discount|enterprise pricing|pricing)/, "category": "T11"}, {"code": "det-attach-001", "re": /(see attached|attachment|attached (is|are|please|the|for))/, "category": "T14"}];

const RULES_STRONG = [{"code": "det-booking-001", "re": /(book a time|send me your calendar|schedule a call|calendar link|calendly|free (on|at) \w+|what times work)/, "category": "T3"}, {"code": "det-referral-001", "re": /(please contact|reach out to|talk to .* instead|forward you to|the right person is|this is handled by)/, "category": "T5"}, {"code": "det-wrong-001", "re": /(wrong person|wrong address|not the right person|not in that role|no longer work here|not with that company)/, "category": "T10"}];

const RE_NOT_INTERESTED = /(not interested|no thanks|we'?re all set|not the right fit|thanks but no thanks|we already use)/;

const RE_TIMING = /(not right now|maybe later|reach out again|try (q[1-4]|next quarter)|reconnect (next|in))/;

const RE_INFO_REQUEST = /(how does (it|this) work|tell me more|send (me )?(more )?info|case stud|more information|what'?s the process)/;

const RE_POSITIVE_CLEAR = /(happy to talk|let'?s chat|sounds good|i'?d be open to a call|open to a call)/;

const RE_POSITIVE_UNCLEAR = /(interesting|possibly|go on|i may be interested)/;

function matchRule(rules, text, evidence) {
  for (const rule of rules) {
    if (rule.needsEvidence && !evidence) continue;
    if (rule.re.test(text)) return rule;
  }
  return null;
}

function classifyIntakeEvent(event) {
  const ev = event || {};
  const reply = ev.reply || {};
  const text = `${reply.subject || ''} ${reply.text || ''}`.toLowerCase();

  if (ev.malformed === true) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      intake_outcome: 'MALFORMED_PAYLOAD_REJECTED',
      category: null,
      reply_mode: null,
      stop_sequence: false,
      pause_sequence: false,
      suppression_level: 'NONE',
      human_review_required: true,
      escalation_required: false,
      reason_codes: ['malformed_payload'],
    });
  }

  if (ev.event_type && ev.event_type !== 'reply_received') {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      intake_outcome: 'UNSUPPORTED_EVENT_NOOP',
      category: 'NOOP',
      reply_mode: 'NO_REPLY',
      stop_sequence: false,
      pause_sequence: false,
      suppression_level: 'NONE',
      human_review_required: false,
      escalation_required: false,
      reason_codes: ['unsupported_event_type'],
    });
  }

  if (
    ev.is_auto_reply === true ||
    (ev.lead_email && ev.eaccount && String(ev.lead_email).toLowerCase() === String(ev.eaccount).toLowerCase())
  ) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      intake_outcome: 'SELF_SENT_NOOP',
      category: 'NOOP',
      reply_mode: 'NO_REPLY',
      stop_sequence: false,
      pause_sequence: false,
      suppression_level: 'NONE',
      human_review_required: false,
      escalation_required: false,
      reason_codes: ['self_sent_or_auto_reply'],
    });
  }

  if (ev.duplicate_of || ev.duplicate_intake_id) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      intake_outcome: 'DUPLICATE_REJECTED',
      category: null,
      reply_mode: null,
      stop_sequence: false,
      pause_sequence: false,
      suppression_level: 'NONE',
      human_review_required: false,
      escalation_required: false,
      reason_codes: [ev.duplicate_intake_id ? 'repeated_execution_duplicate_intake_id' : 'duplicate_dedupe_key'],
    });
  }

  if (ev.campaign_known === false) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T16,
      intake_outcome: 'CAMPAIGN_UNKNOWN_HUMAN_REVIEW',
      category: 'T16',
      reason_codes: ['campaign_context_unknown'],
    });
  }

  if (!String(reply.text || '').trim() && reply.has_attachments !== true) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T15,
      intake_outcome: 'EMPTY_REPLY_ESCALATE',
      category: 'T15',
      reason_codes: ['empty_reply'],
    });
  }

  let hit = matchRule(RULES_HARD_SAFETY, text, true);
  if (hit) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS[hit.category],
      intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED',
      category: hit.category,
      legal_review_required: hit.legal === true,
      privacy_review_required: hit.privacy === true,
      reputational_review_required: hit.reputational === true,
      reason_codes: [hit.code],
    });
  }

  if (reply.has_attachments === true) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T14,
      intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED',
      category: 'T14',
      reason_codes: ['det-attach-001'],
    });
  }

  hit = matchRule(RULES_BOUNCE_OOO, text, ev.delivery_status_evidence === true);
  if (hit) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS[hit.category],
      intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED',
      category: hit.category,
      reason_codes: [hit.code],
    });
  }

  hit = matchRule(RULES_PRICE_ATTACH, text, true);
  if (hit) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS[hit.category],
      intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED',
      category: hit.category,
      reason_codes: [hit.code],
    });
  }

  hit = matchRule(RULES_STRONG, text, true);
  if (hit) {
    return Object.assign({}, {
      ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS[hit.category],
      intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED',
      category: hit.category,
      reason_codes: [hit.code],
    });
  }

  if (RE_NOT_INTERESTED.test(text)) {
    return Object.assign({}, { ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T6, intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED', category: 'T6', reason_codes: ['not_interested'] });
  }
  if (RE_TIMING.test(text)) {
    return Object.assign({}, { ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T4, intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED', category: 'T4', reason_codes: ['timing_objection'] });
  }
  if (RE_INFO_REQUEST.test(text)) {
    return Object.assign({}, { ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T2, intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED', category: 'T2', reason_codes: ['information_request'] });
  }
  if (RE_POSITIVE_CLEAR.test(text)) {
    return Object.assign({}, { ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T1, intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED', category: 'T1', reply_mode: 'FIXED_TEMPLATE_APPROVAL', reason_codes: ['positive_interest_clear'] });
  }
  if (RE_POSITIVE_UNCLEAR.test(text)) {
    return Object.assign({}, { ...CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T1, intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED', category: 'T1', reply_mode: 'AI_DRAFT_APPROVAL', reason_codes: ['positive_interest_unclear'] });
  }

  // Nothing deterministic matched: fall through to the (mocked) AI
  // classifier category, defaulting to T15 AMBIGUOUS (confidence < 0.60).
  const fallback = ev.mock_ai_category || 'T15';
  return Object.assign({}, {
    ...(CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS[fallback] || CATEGORY_ACTION_DEFAULTS.T15),
    intake_outcome: 'ACCEPTED',
    category: fallback,
    reason_codes: ['ai_classifier_fallback'],
  });
}

function getPath(obj, pathStr) {
  return String(pathStr)
    .split('.')
    .reduce((acc, key) => (acc === null || acc === undefined ? undefined : acc[key]), obj);
}

function compareExpected(actual, expected) {
  const diffs = [];
  for (const [key, expectedValue] of Object.entries(expected || {})) {
    const actualValue = getPath(actual, key);
    if (JSON.stringify(actualValue) !== JSON.stringify(expectedValue)) {
      diffs.push({ path: key, expected: expectedValue, actual: actualValue });
    }
  }
  return Object.assign({}, { passed: diffs.length === 0, diffs });
}

function runFixture(fixture, deps) {
  const checkFn = CHECKS[fixture.check];
  if (!checkFn) {
    return Object.assign({}, { id: fixture.id, group: fixture.group, passed: false, error: `unknown check: ${fixture.check}` });
  }
  try {
    const actual = checkFn(fixture, deps);
    if (actual && actual.skipped === true) {
      return Object.assign({}, { id: fixture.id, group: fixture.group, passed: true, skipped: true, note: actual.note });
    }
    const { passed, diffs } = compareExpected(actual, fixture.expected || {});
    return passed
      ? { id: fixture.id, group: fixture.group, passed: true }
      : { id: fixture.id, group: fixture.group, passed: false, diffs };
  } catch (error) {
    return Object.assign({}, { id: fixture.id, group: fixture.group, passed: false, error: error && error.message ? error.message : String(error) });
  }
}

function runFixtureMatrix(fixtures, deps) {
  const list = Array.isArray(fixtures) ? fixtures : [];
  const results = list.map((fixture) => runFixture(fixture, deps));
  const passed = results.filter((r) => r.passed).length;
  const failed = results.length - passed;
  return Object.assign({}, {
    schema_version: '1.0',
    synthetic: true,
    total: results.length,
    passed,
    failed,
    overall_result: failed === 0 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL',
    results,
  });
}

const CHECKS = {
  "classify_intake": (fixture) => classifyIntakeEvent(fixture.input),
  "sender_gates": (fixture, deps) => deps.sc.runSendGates(deps.sc.validateSenderInput(fixture.input)),
  "sender_gate_rejection": (fixture, deps) => {
    const gated = deps.sc.runSendGates(deps.sc.validateSenderInput(fixture.input));
    return deps.sc.buildGateRejectionTerminal(gated);
  },
  "sender_dry_run_terminal": (fixture, deps) => {
    const gated = deps.sc.runSendGates(deps.sc.validateSenderInput(fixture.input));
    const keyed = deps.sc.computeSendKey(gated);
    const acquired = deps.sc.normalizeAcquisitionResult(keyed, { acquired: true, sendKey: 'b'.repeat(64), state: 'LOCKED' });
    const suppressed = deps.sc.mockSuppressionAdapter(acquired);
    const verified = deps.sc.verifySuppressionResults(suppressed);
    return deps.sc.buildDryRunTerminal(verified, { state: 'DRY_RUN_OK' });
  },
  "acquisition_terminal": (fixture, deps) => {
    const gated = deps.sc.runSendGates(deps.sc.validateSenderInput(fixture.input.decisionOutput));
    const keyed = deps.sc.computeSendKey(gated);
    const normalized = deps.sc.normalizeAcquisitionResult(keyed, fixture.input.acquireResponse);
    return deps.sc.buildBlockedDuplicateTerminal(normalized);
  },
  "suppression": (fixture, deps) => {
    const gated = deps.sc.runSendGates(deps.sc.validateSenderInput(fixture.input));
    const suppressed = deps.sc.mockSuppressionAdapter(gated);
    const verified = deps.sc.verifySuppressionResults(suppressed);
    if (verified.suppression_verification.verified === false) {
      return deps.sc.buildSuppressionEscalationTerminal(verified);
    }
    return verified;
  },
  "send_outcome": (fixture, deps) => deps.sc.classifySendOutcome(fixture.input.outcome),
  "plan_attempts": (fixture, deps) => deps.sc.planSendAttempts(fixture.input.outcomes, fixture.input.options || {}),
  "reconcile": (fixture, deps) => deps.sc.reconcileMatches(fixture.input.checks, fixture.input.options || {}),
  "watchdog_evaluation": (fixture, deps) => deps.wc.evaluateUnfinishedRecords(fixture.input).watchdog_evaluation,
  "error_record_sanitised": (fixture, deps) => deps.ec.redactErrorRecord(deps.ec.normalizeErrorEvent(fixture.input)).error_record,
  "placeholder_routing": (fixture, deps) => {
    const evaluated = deps.wc.evaluateUnfinishedRecords(fixture.input);
    const withAlerts = deps.wc.buildAlertRecords(evaluated);
    return deps.wc.mergeAlertDedupeResults(withAlerts, fixture.input.dedupeResponse).notification;
  },
  "send_uncertain_not_retryable": (fixture, deps) => deps.ec.normalizeErrorEvent(fixture.input).error_record,
  "sidecar_resolved_exclusion": () => ({ skipped: true, note: 'verified against infrastructure/send-state/state-store.mjs in run-offline-tests.mjs' }),
  "sidecar_alert_dedupe": () => ({ skipped: true, note: 'verified against infrastructure/send-state/state-store.mjs in run-offline-tests.mjs' })
};

const sc = {
  normalizeEmail,
  djb2Hash,
  deriveSendKey,
  isValidSentContract,
  deterministicJitter,
  validateSenderInput,
  runSendGates,
  buildGateRejectionTerminal,
  computeSendKey,
  normalizeAcquisitionResult,
  buildBlockedDuplicateTerminal,
  mockSuppressionAdapter,
  verifySuppressionResults,
  buildSuppressionEscalationTerminal,
  buildDryRunTerminal,
  classifySendOutcome,
  planSendAttempts,
  reconcileMatches,
};
const ec = {
  redactValue,
  classifyErrorClass,
  deriveRetryable,
  deriveOperatorAction,
  normalizeErrorEvent,
  redactErrorRecord,
  buildPlaceholderNotification,
  attachErrorId,
};
const wc = {
  classifySendRecord,
  classifyErrorRecord,
  sloTypeForCategory,
  timestampForSendRecord,
  ageSeconds,
  statusForAge,
  evaluateUnfinishedRecords,
  buildAlertKey,
  buildAlertRecords,
  mergeAlertDedupeResults,
  buildWatchdogResult,
  mergeUnfinishedResponse,
  attachPhase4bResultId,
};

const harness_result = runFixtureMatrix(FIXTURES, { sc, ec, wc });

return [{ json: { schema_version: '1.0', synthetic: true, validation_mode: true, harness_result } }];

## A. Embedded Fixture Matrix

60 fixtures embedded as a JSON literal from fixtures/phase_4/{intake,sender,failure,monitoring}_fixtures.json:

- intake (policy/intake): 23

parameters.content:
# HMZ - Instantly Reply Intake - Validation (Phase 3)

WHAT: Synthetic webhook intake -> configuration gate -> payload validation -> NES normalization -> idempotency check -> deterministic prefilter -> handoff to the Reply Decision Engine sub-workflow (workflow NJcnNQoJ5nSIWYte). Returns the normalized event plus the Decision Engine's decision, draft, and validation result.

WHY: Implements docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 3 for HMZ's own US B2B validation campaign. OPERATING_MODE=VALIDATION, DRY_RUN=true, LIVE_CAMPAIGNS=[].

MOCKED: No connection to Instantly, any AI provider, email infrastructure, Slack, Sheets, or Supabase anywhere in this workflow. The webhook accepts only local synthetic POST payloads from fixtures/phase_3/.

CONFIGURE LATER: Replace the dev webhook path and payload shape with the real Instantly reply webhook once a payload is captured (docs/INSTANTLY_FIELD_MAP.md). Provision idempotency storage (n8n Data Tables). Resolve docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_UNKNOWNS.md section D owner decisions (sender name / booking link mappings, storage choice, escalation owners).

HOW TO TEST: Keep this workflow INACTIVE. In the editor, use 'Listen for test event' on the Webhook node, then POST a fixtures/phase_3/*.json file to the shown loopback test URL. Inspect each node's output.

EXPECTED OUTPUT: one item per inbound event containing intake_id, received_at, raw_payload, the validated/normalized NES object, idempotency_state, prefilter result, and (where forwarded) the Decision Engine's decision / draft / validation fields. No external side effects.

ANALOGY: A hospital intake desk - time-stamps incoming paperwork, checks the form is filled in, assigns a file number, checks it is not a duplicate visit, and forwards the file to triage (the Decision Engine). It does not diagnose or treat anyone itself.

# Section A - Synthetic Webhook Intake

WHAT: A dev-only Webhook node accepts a synthetic POST payload shaped like an Instantly 'reply_received' event. The Code node immediately preserves the full raw payload, records received_at, and assigns an intake_id (reusing body.intake_id if a fixture supplies one, for deterministic duplicate testing).

WHY: Establishes a single, time-stamped, traceable entry point with a stable internal identifier before any validation or normalization happens (spec section A).

MOCKED: The webhook path ('hmz-validation-reply-intake-dev') and payload shape are PROVISIONAL development placeholders, not the real Instantly webhook. responseMode=onReceived so the webhook acknowledges immediately (Processing SLO) without waiting for downstream sections.

CONFIGURE LATER: Replace path/shape with the real Instantly reply webhook once docs/INSTANTLY_FIELD_MAP.md is confirmed against a captured payload. Add the compensating controls from docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_UNKNOWNS.md B4 (secret path, allowlisting, strict validation) before any non-loopback exposure. Confirm whether Instantly supplies its own event/email IDs that should take priority over this generated intake_id.

HOW TO TEST: With the workflow open (INACTIVE), click 'Listen for test event' on the Webhook node, then POST any fixtures/phase_3/*.json file to the displayed loopback test URL (never expose this port).

EXPECTED OUTPUT: one item with intake_id (string), received_at (ISO timestamp), raw_payload (the exact posted body, untouched), synthetic: true.

ANALOGY: The front-desk clerk who time-stamps every incoming envelope and staples a tracking number to it before it goes anywhere else. The clerk does not read or judge the contents.

# Section B - Configuration Gate

WHAT: Attaches a hardcoded validation-mode CONFIG object to every item (operating_mode=VALIDATION, dry_run=true, live_campaigns=[], geo_code=US_B2B_CORE_12, the 3 approved validation cells, allowed_country=US, region_priority=[EASTERN, CENTRAL], transmission_disabled=true, external_actions_mocked=true). Then checks raw_payload for any claim that would require a live send (campaign_id in LIVE_CAMPAIGNS, raw_payload.dry_run=false, raw_payload.operating_mode != VALIDATION, or raw_payload.live_campaign=true).

WHY: spec section B and CLAUDE.md rule 5 - the workflow must fail safely if dry-run is not true or a synthetic event tries to claim a live campaign.

MOCKED: CONFIG is a hardcoded constant for Phase 3, not read from n8n environment variables or an external config store.

CONFIGURE LATER: Move CONFIG to environment-variable-backed values once a config storage adapter is chosen (docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_UNKNOWNS.md section D).

BRANCHING (Phase 3.1A repair, resolves reports/UNRESOLVED_ITEMS.md U2): immediately downstream is B1. Configuration Gate Router, a native IF node with condition config_gate.passed === true. If true, the item continues to C. Payload Validation exactly as before. If false, the item is routed to B2. Configuration Gate Rejection (Terminal), which sets terminal_status=REJECTED, processing_halted=true, external_action_status=NOOP, and a reason built from config_gate.reasons; B2 is the FINAL output for that item - Sections C-G (normalization, idempotency, prefilter, Decision Engine) never run. Because CONFIG.dry_run=true and live_campaigns=[] are hardcoded, config_gate.passed is always true for realistic Phase 3 inputs, so the false branch is exercised only by fixture 14 (raw_payload.live_campaign=true).

HOW TO TEST: POST any of the 12 standard fixtures and confirm config_gate.passed=true, dry_run_ok=true, live_campaign_claim_detected=false, and the item proceeds to C. POST fixture 14 (raw_payload.live_campaign=true) and confirm config_gate.passed=false, the item is routed to B2, and the final output has terminal_status=REJECTED, processing_halted=true, external_action_status=NOOP, with no nes/idempotency/prefilter/decision/classifier/validation fields present.

Missing or empty fields become null with a structured validation_errors[] entry. If raw_payload itself is missing, not an object, an array, or lacks a non-empty event_type, the whole payload is MALFORMED.

Second execution: idempotency.is_duplicate=true, the item is routed to E4(false)->E5, and the final output has terminal_status=COMPLETED_NO_SEND, processing_halted=true, external_action_status=NOOP, stop_active_sequence=false, with prefilter/deterministic/classifier/decision/draft/validation all absent. POST the malformed-payload fixture (#11) and a DIFFERENT malformed payload (fixture #13) and confirm both get key_scheme='identifier_poor_hash' with DIFFERENT idempotency_key values (different payload_hash), and both are processed as op-malformed/REJECTED rather than either being misread as a duplicate of the other.

EXPECTED OUTPUT: a non-duplicate item gains idempotency{idempotency_key, key_scheme, key_components{event_id, email_id, campaign_id, email_account, lead_email}, proposed_state, state_enum[9], storage_adapter, storage_table_name, is_duplicate=false, table_row_id, created_at, updated_at, duplicate_detection_method, duplicate_detection_status} and continues through E4(true) to F/G. A duplicate item gets idempotency.is_duplicate=true and E4(false) routes it to E5 as the workflow's final output for that item.

ANALOGY: A hospital admissions desk that builds a file number from patient ID + visit reason + date - or, if the patient cannot be identified, a fingerprint of the intake form itself, so two different unidentified patients still get different file numbers - checks its card catalogue for that exact card, files or re-files it, and - if this is a repeat of a visit already on file - sends the patient straight back to the waiting room with a "you're already checked in" note instead of starting a new triage.

# Section F - Deterministic Prefilter

WHAT: Computes 11 deterministic operational flags from nes, idempotency, and payload_status using simple keyword/regex checks against reply.subject/text/snippet: is_unsupported_event_type, is_malformed_payload, is_duplicate_event, is_self_sent, is_empty_reply, is_automated_response, is_out_of_office, is_bounce, is_unsubscribe_language, has_attachments, campaign_cell_mismatch.

WHY: spec section F - supplies the Decision Engine's deterministic policy stage (Workflow 2 section A) with the prefilter.* fields its opFlags and det-* rules read (is_unsupported_event_type, is_malformed_payload, is_duplicate_event, is_self_sent, is_empty_reply, is_automated_response, campaign_cell_mismatch, plus is_out_of_office/is_bounce/is_unsubscribe_language/has_attachments for the det-ooo/det-bounce/det-unsub/det-attach rules).

MOCKED: keyword/regex detection only - is_automated_response, is_out_of_office, is_bounce, is_unsubscribe_language are substring/regex matches on subject+text+snippet (plus event_type), not a header-based or ML classifier. SUPPORTED_EVENT_TYPES = reply_received, auto_reply_received, email_bounced, lead_unsubscribed (docs/INSTANTLY_FIELD_MAP.md section 2.1, the verified list of Instantly event types this responder is designed to act on). These flags are operational evidence only, not semantic certainty - final category assignment happens in Workflow 2.

CONFIGURE LATER: Tune/expand the regex patterns once real auto-reply, OOO, bounce, and unsubscribe wording is observed from Instantly/ESPs.

HOW TO TEST: POST the out-of-office fixture (#5) and confirm prefilter.is_out_of_office=true. POST the bounce fixture (#6) and confirm prefilter.is_bounce=true (and is_unsupported_event_type=false). POST the unsubscribe fixture (#4) and confirm prefilter.is_unsubscribe_language=true.

EXPECTED OUTPUT: each item gains prefilter{is_unsupported_event_type, is_malformed_payload, is_duplicate_event, is_self_sent, is_empty_reply, is_automated_response, is_out_of_office, is_bounce, is_unsubscribe_language, has_attachments, campaign_cell_mismatch} (all boolean).

ANALOGY: A triage nurse running a quick checklist (breathing, bleeding, conscious) before the doctor (the Decision Engine) makes any diagnosis - fast and deterministic, never claiming a diagnosis itself.

# Section G - Decision Engine Handoff

WHAT: An Execute Sub-workflow node calls 'HMZ - Reply Decision Engine - Validation' (workflow NJcnNQoJ5nSIWYte) once per item, passing the entire current item (intake_id, received_at, raw_payload, synthetic, config, config_gate, validated, validation_errors, payload_status, nes, idempotency, prefilter) through to the sub-workflow's passthrough trigger. The sub-workflow's 5 sections (A deterministic policy, B mock classifier, C decision policy, D mock draft, E output validation) each spread {...input, ...}, so the item returned here is this same item plus deterministic{}, classifier{}, decision{}, validation{}.

WHY: spec section G - pass the normalized event to the Reply Decision Engine using the safest supported sub-workflow pattern, and preserve the structured decision result. Execute Sub-workflow by workflow ID (source=database) is the native n8n mechanism; waitForSubWorkflow=true (default) keeps this synchronous so the decision is available on this workflow's final output.

MOCKED: the sub-workflow's classifier (section B) and draft preparation (section D) are themselves mocked/provider-neutral (no real AI, no Instantly call) - see workflow NJcnNQoJ5nSIWYte's own sticky notes.

CONFIGURE LATER: nothing Instantly-specific - this is a pure n8n-to-n8n call. If a real AI provider is later wired into the Decision Engine, this handoff node does not need to change.

HOW TO TEST: POST any of the 12 fixtures and confirm the final output item contains decision.category, decision.terminal_status, decision.reply_permitted, decision.external_action_status (always NOT_PERFORMED or NOOP), draft.template_id, and validation.valid=true.

EXPECTED OUTPUT: the full item from Section F, plus deterministic{}, classifier{}, decision{} (19 required fields), draft{}, validation{valid, errors[], checked_at}. This is the FINAL output of Workflow 1 for Phase 3 - no sender, SLA watchdog, or error workflow exists yet (out of scope).

ANALOGY: The triage nurse hands the completed chart to the attending physician (the Decision Engine) and waits at the door for the diagnosis and care plan to be clipped back onto the same chart, then carries the completed chart back to the front desk. No treatment is administered here.

# HMZ - Instantly Reply Sender - Validation (Phase 4A)

WHAT: Sub-workflow invoked with the Reply Decision Engine output (workflow NJcnNQoJ5nSIWYte). Re-validates required identifiers, re-runs every send/suppression/safety gate, derives a stable send key, acquires durable ownership from the hmz-send-state sidecar, runs suppression actions via a mock adapter only, and - because DRY_RUN=true is hardcoded - transitions to DRY_RUN_OK and returns one structured terminal object. No external HTTP call to Instantly is ever made.

HARDCODED: OPERATING_MODE=VALIDATION, DRY_RUN=true, LIVE_CAMPAIGNS=[], LIVE_CREDENTIAL_READY=false. No input path may override these.

SIDECAR: http://hmz-send-state:5681 (internal Docker network only, not published to the host). Provides atomic send-ownership and forward-only send state. See infrastructure/send-state/README.md.

INACTIVE: this workflow must remain inactive after import and after every test.

parameters.content:
## D-I. Send Key, Ownership, Suppression

D computes a stable send key from the canonical inbound Email ID, sender, recipient and the resolved reply_template_id - never from a random body marker. E POSTs to hmz-send-state /v1/send/acquire (internal Docker network only). A concurrent second acquisition for the same identity returns acquired=false, reason=LOCK_ALREADY_HELD; a later sequential rerun returns reason=DURABLE_STATE_EXISTS. F routes blocked results to a terminal no-send result (F2) without any further action.

G runs only a mock suppression adapter (source-campaign action plus exact workspace email block-list action) - no live Instantly call. H verifies both actions; any partial/uncertain result escalates (I2) and is never sent as an unsubscribe confirmation.

## J-N. DRY_RUN Gate and Live Adapter Contract

J checks gates.dry_run_gate_passed, which is true only when SENDER_CONFIG.DRY_RUN === true (hardcoded). K transitions the hmz-send-state record from LOCKED to DRY_RUN_OK (a terminal state) and K2 returns one structured terminal object with sent=false, transport=NONE.

N (Live Adapter Contract) is wired only to the J "false" branch, which is never taken because DRY_RUN is hardcoded true - it documents the verified V3 POST /api/v2/emails/reply contract (eaccount, reply_to_uuid, subject, body.text/html) plus the V5 retry/classification (classifySendOutcome, planSendAttempts) and read-only reconciliation (reconcileMatches) contracts as deterministic code, without binding credentials or making any HTTP request. LIVE_CREDENTIAL_READY=false and LIVE_CAMPAIGNS=[] additionally block it if it were ever reached.

parameters.content:
## Reply Decision Engine - Validation (Phase 3)

**What:** Sub-workflow. Receives one normalized reply event (NES v1) plus deterministic prefilter flags from "HMZ - Instantly Reply Intake - Validation" via Execute Workflow. Returns a single structured decision object (action plan) per policy-HMZ-1.2. Performs NO external actions.

**Why:** Centralises the 16-category reply policy (T1-T16) so the intake workflow stays a thin normalization layer. Keeps deterministic safety rules (Section A) authoritative over the mock classifier (Section B).

**Mocked / not real:**
- Section B "semantic classifier" is deterministic keyword heuristics standing in for a future AI call. model="mock". No external AI provider is called.
- No Instantly, email, Slack, Sheets, Supabase, or DB calls anywhere in this workflow.
- All suppression / DNC / send / escalation actions are returned as structured INTENTS only - nothing is transmitted.

**Configure later:**
- Replace Section B with a real provider-neutral classifier call (must still be overridden by Section A deterministic results).
- Wire suppression intents to verified Instantly endpoints once docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_UNKNOWNS.md B6-B9 are resolved.
- Approve KB-1.0 and policy-HMZ-1.2 before any live use.

**How to test:** Open this workflow, click "Execute workflow", and use "Pin data" on the trigger node with the JSON from one of fixtures/phase_3/*.json (the `decision_engine_input` field). Inspect the final item's JSON for the decision object.

**Expected output:** One JSON item containing `category`, `confidence`, `evidence`, `detected_questions`, `requested_action`, `risk_flags`, `permitted_automation_action`, validation-learning fields, `interest_stage`, plus the action-plan fields from Sections C/D/E (stop_active_sequence, durable_dnc_intent, address_suppression_intent, review_hold, ... reply_template_id, reply_draft_status, ... terminal_status, reason).

**Analogy:** A triage desk inside a clinic back office. A note arrives describing a patient's message; the desk decides "close this file", "flag for the doctor", or "draft a reply for the doctor to sign" - and writes that decision on a card. The desk never contacts the patient itself.

## A. Deterministic Policy Stage

**What:** Applies the hard-coded keyword/pattern rules from policy-HMZ-1.2 Section 5 (det-unsub-001 ... det-wrong-001) plus the operational prefilter flags from Workflow 1 Section F (duplicate, self-sent, malformed, unsupported event type, empty reply). Produces `deterministic.flags`, `deterministic.media_flag`, `deterministic.operational_flags`, `deterministic.category`, `deterministic.rule_id`, `deterministic.deterministic_match`, `deterministic.noop_match`.

**Why:** policy-HMZ-1.2 requires deterministic safety rules to run BEFORE and OVERRIDE any semantic classifier result. Hard-safety categories (T7 unsubscribe, T8 OOO, T9 bounce, T11 pricing, T12 legal/privacy/complaint, T13 hostile, T14 attachment, T3 booking, T5 referral, T10 wrong person) are resolved here without AI.

**Mocked / not real:** Nothing external is called. All matching is local regex/keyword logic on `nes.reply.subject` / `nes.reply.text` / `nes.reply.from_address_email` and the `prefilter` flags. `det-media-001` is tracked only as `media_flag` - per policy it must NOT auto-classify as hostile.

**Configure later:** Refine keyword lists to match approved policy phrasing as policy-HMZ-1.x evolves. This stage must remain authoritative over Section B.

**How to test:** Pin a fixture's `decision_engine_input` (containing `nes` + `prefilter`) on the trigger node, execute, and inspect `deterministic.category` / `deterministic.rule_id` on this node's output.

**Expected output:** Same item plus a `deterministic` object. `category=null` only when no Section 5 rule and no operational flag matched (item passes to Section B). `noop_match=true` for duplicate/self-sent items (no further classification needed).

**Analogy:** A hospital triage nurse checks a fixed list of red-flag symptoms before any doctor sees the patient. If a red flag is found, that determines the track immediately - the doctor's later opinion cannot override it.

## B. Deterministic Reply Classifier

**What:** Deterministic, provider-neutral heuristic classifier (`model: "deterministic-heuristic-1.0"`). If Section A already produced a deterministic or noop match, passes that through unchanged with `confidence: 1`. Otherwise applies simple keyword heuristics for the remaining semantic categories: T6 NOT_INTERESTED, T4 TIMING_OBJECTION, T2 INFORMATION_REQUEST, T1 POSITIVE_INTEREST, else T15 AMBIGUOUS. Returns `category`, `confidence`, `evidence`, `detected_questions`, `requested_action`, `risk_flags`, `permitted_automation_action`, `interest_stage`, `validation_learning`.

**Why:** policy-HMZ-1.2 Section 3 needs T1/T2/T4/T6/T15 distinguished where no hard rule applies. Keeping this as a separate stage means a real provider-neutral model can replace the keyword logic later without changing Section A or the output contract.

**Heuristic / not an AI model:** No AI provider is called. `kb_reference: "KB-1.0-DRAFT"` and `thread_context: "NOT_AVAILABLE_PHASE_3"` are placeholders only - no real KB excerpt or thread history is fetched. Deterministic keyword matches never exceed confidence 0.75, so per Section 4 confidence bands they can never alone authorise a send (band 0.60-0.899 = retain, block auto, escalate). T16 OTHER is never produced here - it only arises from Section A's malformed/unsupported operational flags.

**Configure later:** Replace `deterministicHeuristicClassify` with a real provider-neutral model call that receives the same inputs (`nes`, `deterministic`, `classifierInputs`) and returns the same shape. Wire a real KB excerpt lookup keyed by `kb_reference` and `validation_cell`.

**How to test:** Run after Section A with a pinned fixture. For fixtures with no det-rule match (e.g. positive interest, info request, ambiguous one-word reply), confirm `classifier.category`/`confidence` match the fixture's expected category. For fixtures with a det-rule match, confirm passthrough at confidence 1.

**Expected output:** Same item plus a `classifier` object with all fields above. `validation_learning` carries forward Workflow 1's `nes.validation_learning_seed` defaults (`unknown`), with `voice_of_customer_excerpt` filled from the reply text and `pricing_interest` set to `true` only for PRICING_OR_COMMERCIAL_NEGOTIATION. This `classifier.validation_learning` object is the enriched, per-execution result and becomes the workflow's single authoritative `validation_learning` (exposed at the top level by Section E).

**Analogy:** A junior associate who has already been told the firm's hard-and-fast rules (Section A). For everything else, they skim the letter and make a provisional, low-confidence guess at what kind of letter it is - then hand it to a partner (Section C) for the actual decision.

## C. Decision Policy

**What:** Converts `category` + `deterministic` flags + `classifier` output into the structured action-plan object `decision`, per policy-HMZ-1.2 Sections 3.1/7/7.1. Contains stop_active_sequence, durable_dnc_intent, address_suppression_intent, review_hold, legal/privacy/reputational_review_required, human_review_required, reply_permitted, reply_template_id, reply_draft_status, external_action_status, follow_up_date, interest_stage, terminal_status, reason, plus a few additional fields (pause_active_sequence, data_cleanup_required, interest_status_update, referred_person_contact_permitted, confidence_overridden) that the Phase 3 spec's minimum list does not exclude.

**Why:** Centralises the per-category action matrix in one lookup table so every category's handling is visible and auditable in one place, and so Section E can validate the result against a single shape.

**Mocked / not real:** No suppression, DNC, blocklist, or send action is performed - durable_dnc_intent / address_suppression_intent / external_action_status are intents only (`external_action_status` is always NOT_PERFORMED or NOOP). T12/T13 always get `review_hold:true` and `terminal_status:REVIEW_HOLD`. Confidence < 0.60 overrides to AMBIGUOUS (policy Section 4). `det-media-001` forces `reputational_review_required:true` (P3M track) without changing `category`. T1 never sets `interest_stage` to ALPHA_INTEREST (clamped to VALIDATION_SPRINT_INTEREST if a future classifier ever returns it).

**Configure later:** Replace template IDs (T1_SCENARIO_A_OPEN_TO_CALL, T1_SCENARIO_C_UNCLEAR_INTEREST, T2_KB_INFORMATION_REQUEST, T4_SCENARIO_B_VAGUE, T5_REFERRAL_ACK, T6_NOT_INTERESTED_ACK, T7_UNSUBSCRIBE_CONFIRMATION, T10_WRONG_PERSON_ACK) with the final approved template registry IDs once confirmed. Wire durable_dnc_intent/address_suppression_intent to a verified Instantly suppression mechanism once B6-B9 are resolved (docs/ASSUMPTIONS_AND_UNKNOWNS.md).

**How to test:** Run after Sections A/B with a pinned fixture and confirm `decision.terminal_status`, `decision.review_hold`, `decision.reply_permitted`, and `decision.reply_template_id` match the fixture's expected values.

**Expected output:** Same item plus a `decision` object containing every field listed above. `external_action_status` is never anything but NOT_PERFORMED or NOOP. `terminal_status` is one of REVIEW_HOLD, COMPLETED_NO_SEND, or REJECTED (malformed payload only).

**Analogy:** The clinic's senior partner reads the triage note and the junior associate's guess, then writes the actual instruction on the patient's chart: "hold for my review", "file as closed, no follow-up needed", or "prepare a draft letter for me to sign" - never "send this now".

## D. Mock Draft Preparation

**What:** For categories with a fixed-template `reply_template_id` (T1 Scenario A/C, T4 Scenario B, T5, T6, T7, T10), substitutes `<<firstName>>`, `<<senderName>>`, `<<bookingLink>>` placeholders into the approved wording from policy-HMZ-1.2 Section 6 and returns a `draft` object: `template_id`, `draft_text`, `draft_status`, `missing_variables`, `human_review_required`, `notes`. For T2 (information request) and all human-only/no-template categories, returns `draft_text: null` with an explanatory note - never fabricated KB content.

**Why:** Lets a human reviewer see exactly what would be sent and what is still missing, without ever transmitting anything, per the Phase 3 mock-draft-preparation requirement.

**Mocked / not real:** No knowledge base, AI provider, or template registry service is called. `SENDER_CONFIG` (per-account `senderName`/`bookingLink`) is intentionally empty - those mappings are an open owner decision (ASSUMPTIONS_AND_UNKNOWNS.md D7), so `senderName` and `bookingLink` are always reported in `missing_variables` for now and shown as bracketed placeholders in the draft text. `<<firstName>>` reads an optional `nes.lead_first_name` field that is not part of NES v1 Section 1 and may not be populated by Workflow 1 yet; if absent or rejected by the Section 9 validation rules, the approved no-name variant is used where one exists, otherwise a bracketed placeholder is shown. Campaign-status wording uses the conservative "US B2B teams" phrasing (not "a small number of") until participants have entered validation, per the Section 6 wording rule.

**Configure later:** Populate `SENDER_CONFIG` with owner-approved senderName/bookingLink per `eaccount` once D7 is resolved. Confirm whether/how Workflow 1 will populate `nes.lead_first_name`. Convert `<<var>>` tokens to the real mustache placeholder form only inside an eventual transmission layer, never inside this Code node (n8n Code nodes reject literal double-curly-brace text).

**How to test:** Run a fixture through Sections A-D. For T1/T4/T5/T6/T7/T10 categories confirm `draft.draft_text` is non-null, contains no unresolved `<<...>>` tokens, and lists `senderName`/`bookingLink`/`firstName` in `missing_variables` as appropriate. For T2 and all human-only categories confirm `draft.draft_text` is null and `draft.notes` explains why.

**Expected output:** Same item plus a `draft` object. `draft_text` is either null or a plain-text message with blank-line-separated paragraphs and no unresolved tokens (unresolved values use `[[name: ...]]` markers instead). `draft.draft_text` is never transmitted by this or any later Phase 3 node.

**Analogy:** A paralegal fills in a standard letter template with whatever client details are on file, circles the blanks they could not fill in, and leaves the finished draft on the partner's desk for sign-off. The letter never goes in the post on its own.

## E. Output Validation

**What:** Final safety-net check on `decision` (and `draft` / `classifier.validation_learning`) before the result leaves Workflow 2: confirms all 19 required `decision` fields are present, `category` is one of the 16 approved values or null, `confidence` is 0-1, all boolean fields are strictly true/false, the 9 validation-learning fields are true/false/"unknown", `address_suppression_intent` / `terminal_status` / `reply_draft_status` / `external_action_status` use only allowed enum values, `interest_stage` is never `ALPHA_INTEREST`, the draft text contains none of a fixed list of prohibited-claim patterns (guarantee, proven, case study, testimonial, price, "20+", results, established), and `reply_permitted=true` always pairs with `human_review_required=true` under OPERATING_MODE=VALIDATION.

**Why:** This is the structural backstop the Phase 3 spec requires: invalid decision output must route to REVIEW_HOLD, and human review can never be bypassed in validation mode, regardless of whether earlier sections behaved correctly.

**Mocked / not real:** No external service is called. The prohibited-claim scan is a fixed regex list, not an AI or content-safety classifier - it is a deterministic backstop, not a substitute for human review of the draft text.

**Configure later:** Extend `PROHIBITED_CLAIM_PATTERNS` if the approved knowledge base or reply rules add new prohibited phrasing. Revisit the `ALLOWED_*` enums if `docs/HMZ_APPROVED_REPLY_RULES.md` adds new suppression levels, terminal statuses, or draft statuses.

To exercise the failure path, manually corrupt a `decision` field (for example set `external_action_status` to "SENT") on a test item before Section E and confirm `validation.valid` becomes false, `decision.terminal_status` becomes REVIEW_HOLD, `decision.review_hold` and `human_review_required` become true, and `decision.reply_permitted` becomes false.

On failure, `decision` is overwritten with review_hold=true, human_review_required=true, reply_permitted=false, external_action_status=NOT_PERFORMED, terminal_status=REVIEW_HOLD, and an updated `reason`. This is the final node of Workflow 2 - its output is what Workflow 1 Section G receives back from the sub-workflow call.
